1-naive.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries — Colorectal Cancer Diagnoses

### ICD-10-CM Based Queries

```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C18&_include=Condition:subject
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C19&_include=Condition:subject
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C20&_include=Condition:subject
```

### SNOMED CT Based Queries

```
Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|363414004&_include=Condition:subject
Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|363416002&_include=Condition:subject
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|93771007&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### Code Coverage Reference

| Query | Code | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| ICD-10-CM | C18 (`:below`) | Malignant neoplasm of colon — covers C18.0–C18.9 (cecum, ascending, transverse, descending, sigmoid, etc.) |
| ICD-10-CM | C19 | Malignant neoplasm of rectosigmoid junction |
| ICD-10-CM | C20 | Malignant neoplasm of rectum |
| SNOMED CT | 363414004 (`:below`) | Malignant tumour of colon + all descendants |
| SNOMED CT | 363416002 (`:below`) | Malignant tumour of rectum + all descendants |
| SNOMED CT | 93771007 | Primary malignant neoplasm of rectosigmoid junction |

### Notes
- **`:below` modifier** uses subsumption to match parent codes and all hierarchical descendants — recommended for servers supporting terminology subsumption (e.g., HAPI FHIR, Azure Health Data Services).
- **`_include=Condition:subject`** retrieves the associated `Patient` resources alongside each matching `Condition`.
- For servers **not supporting `:below`**, replace the C18 query with explicit subcodes: `C18.0, C18.1, C18.2, C18.3, C18.4, C18.5, C18.6, C18.7, C18.8, C18.9`.
- Add `&verification-status=confirmed` to restrict to confirmed diagnoses if needed.

### ICD-10-CM Queries

**Malignant Neoplasm of Colon (C18.x — all subcategories via `:below`):**
```

```

**Rectosigmoid Junction (C19) & Rectum (C20):**
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C19,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C20&_include=Condition:patient
```

### SNOMED CT Query
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|363414004,http://snomed.info/sct|93761005,http://snomed.info/sct|363418001,http://snomed.info/sct|363353009&_include=Condition:patient
```

---

### Notes

| Code System | Code | Description |
|---|---|---|
| ICD-10-CM | C18 (+ subcodes) | Malignant neoplasm of colon (cecum, ascending, transverse, descending, sigmoid, etc.) |
| ICD-10-CM | C19 | Malignant neoplasm of rectosigmoid junction |
| ICD-10-CM | C20 | Malignant neoplasm of rectum |
| SNOMED CT | 363414004 | Malignant neoplasm of colon |
| SNOMED CT | 93761005 | Primary malignant neoplasm of colon |
| SNOMED CT | 363418001 | Malignant neoplasm of rectum |
| SNOMED CT | 363353009 | Malignant neoplasm of rectosigmoid junction |

- The `code:below` modifier on the ICD-10-CM C18 query uses hierarchical subsumption to capture all colon sub-site codes (C18.0–C18.9) in servers that support it.
- `_include=Condition:patient` returns the linked `Patient` resources in the same Bundle.
- Run all queries and deduplicate results by patient ID for a complete patient list.

### Code Coverage Breakdown

| Query | System | Codes | Covers |
|-------|--------|-------|--------|
| 1 | ICD-10-CM | `C18.0–C18.9` | Malignant neoplasm of colon (all subsites: cecum, ascending, transverse, descending, sigmoid, etc.) |

| 1 | ICD-10-CM | `C20` | Malignant neoplasm of rectum |
| 2 | SNOMED CT | `363414004` + descendants (`:below`) | Malignant tumour of colon (all subtypes) |
| 3 | SNOMED CT | `363415003` | Malignant tumour of rectosigmoid junction |
| 4 | SNOMED CT | `363416002` + descendants (`:below`) | Malignant tumour of rectum (all subtypes) |

### Notes
- **`_include=Condition:subject`** pulls in the associated `Patient` resources in the same bundle response.
- The **`:below` modifier** on SNOMED CT leverages ontological subsumption to capture all child concepts (e.g., adenocarcinoma, squamous cell carcinoma of those sites) without needing to enumerate each code explicitly.

# FHIR Queries: Colorectal Cancer Patient Population Identification

Below are **10 queries** across 4 clinical categories. Collect the union of all `Patient` resources returned (deduplicated by `Patient.id`) to form your CRC QI cohort.

---

## 🔴 CATEGORY 1 — CRC Diagnoses

### 1.1 — ICD-10-CM: All Colorectal Cancer Anatomic Sites
Covers cecum (C18.0), ascending (C18.2), hepatic flexure (C18.3), transverse (C18.4), splenic flexure (C18.5), descending (C18.6), sigmoid (C18.7), overlapping (C18.8), unspecified colon (C18.9), rectosigmoid junction (C19), and rectum (C20):
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C18.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C18.2,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C18.3,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C18.4,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C18.5,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C18.6,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C18.7,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C18.8,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C18.9,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C19,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C20&_include=Condition:patient
```

### 1.2 — SNOMED CT: Colorectal Cancer Concepts
Covers primary colon malignancy, primary rectal malignancy, cecum, ascending, descending, sigmoid colon, overlapping sites, and rectosigmoid:
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|93761005,http://snomed.info/sct|363414004,http://snomed.info/sct|93980000,http://snomed.info/sct|363351006,http://snomed.info/sct|448544006,http://snomed.info/sct|187736009,http://snomed.info/sct|187757001,http://snomed.info/sct|187761004,http://snomed.info/sct|109838007&_include=Condition:patient
```

---

## 💊 CATEGORY 2 — CRC Chemotherapy Medications

### 2.1 — MedicationRequest (Prescriptions)
RxNorm ingredient codes for: 5-FU/fluorouracil (`4492`), capecitabine (`194000`), oxaliplatin (`151932`), irinotecan (`72962`), leucovorin calcium (`27340`):
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|4492,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|194000,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|151932,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|72962,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|27340&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

### 2.2 — MedicationAdministration (Infusion/IV Administration Records)
Same RxNorm codes capturing inpatient/infusion-center administrations (critical for IV 5-FU, oxaliplatin, irinotecan regimens):
```
MedicationAdministration?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|4492,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|194000,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|151932,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|72962,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|27340&_include=MedicationAdministration:patient
```

### 2.3 — MedicationStatement (Patient-Reported or Reconciliation Records)
Captures medications documented outside prescription workflow:
```
MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|4492,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|194000,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|151932,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|72962,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|27340&_include=MedicationStatement:patient
```

---

## 🔬 CATEGORY 3 — Colonoscopy Procedures

### 3.1 — SNOMED CT: Colonoscopy Procedure Concepts
Covers diagnostic colonoscopy (`73761001`), colonoscopy with biopsy (`44441009`), and with polypectomy (`427459009`):
```
Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|73761001,http://snomed.info/sct|44441009,http://snomed.info/sct|427459009&_include=Procedure:patient
```

### 3.2 — CPT: Colonoscopy Procedure Codes
Covers diagnostic (`45378`), with biopsy (`45380`), with submucosal injection (`45381`), bleeding control (`45382`), polyp removal-hot biopsy (`45384`), snare polypectomy (`45385`), dilation (`45386`), ablation (`45388`), endoscopic mucosal resection (`45390`):
```
Procedure?code=http://www.ama-assn.org/go/cpt|45378,http://www.ama-assn.org/go/cpt|45380,http://www.ama-assn.org/go/cpt|45381,http://www.ama-assn.org/go/cpt|45382,http://www.ama-assn.org/go/cpt|45384,http://www.ama-assn.org/go/cpt|45385,http://www.ama-assn.org/go/cpt|45386,http://www.ama-assn.org/go/cpt|45388,http://www.ama-assn.org/go/cpt|45390&_include=Procedure:patient
```

### 3.3 — DiagnosticReport: Colonoscopy Reports
Captures pathology/endoscopy reports associated with colonoscopy findings:
```
DiagnosticReport?code=http://loinc.org|18746-8,http://loinc.org|28054-0&_include=DiagnosticReport:patient
```

---

## 📈 CATEGORY 4 — Elevated CEA (>5 ng/mL)

### 4.1 — Observation: CEA in Serum/Plasma
LOINC `2857-1` (CEA serum/plasma), value > 5 ng/mL (UCUM: `ng/mL`):
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2857-1&value-quantity=gt5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|ng/mL&_include=Observation:patient
```

### 4.2 — Observation: CEA (alternate LOINC/unit — ug/L, numerically equivalent)
Some labs report in µg/L (= ng/mL numerically); include both to avoid missed results:
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2857-1,http://loinc.org|19167-1&value-quantity=gt5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|ug/L&_include=Observation:patient
```

---

## 📊 Query Reference Summary

| # | Query | Resource | Code System | Clinical Meaning |
|---|-------|----------|-------------|-----------------|
| 1.1 | ICD-10-CM CRC Dx | `Condition` | ICD-10-CM | C18.x / C19 / C20 all sites |
| 1.2 | SNOMED CRC Dx | `Condition` | SNOMED CT | Primary colon/rectal malignancy |
| 2.1 | Chemo Prescriptions | `MedicationRequest` | RxNorm | 5-FU, capecitabine, oxaliplatin, irinotecan, leucovorin |
| 2.2 | Chemo Administration | `MedicationAdministration` | RxNorm | IV infused regimens |
| 2.3 | Chemo Statements | `MedicationStatement` | RxNorm | Patient-reported/reconciled |
| 3.1 | Colonoscopy (SNOMED) | `Procedure` | SNOMED CT | Diagnostic & therapeutic colonoscopy |
| 3.2 | Colonoscopy (CPT) | `Procedure` | CPT | Full CPT colonoscopy code range |
| 3.3 | Colonoscopy Reports | `DiagnosticReport` | LOINC | Endoscopy/pathology reports |
| 4.1 | Elevated CEA (ng/mL) | `Observation` | LOINC | CEA > 5 ng/mL in serum |
| 4.2 | Elevated CEA (ug/L) | `Observation` | LOINC | CEA > 5 µg/L (alt unit) |

---

## ⚠️ Implementation Notes

| Concern | Recommendation |
|---|---|
| **Deduplication** | Collect all `Patient.id` values across all result bundles; union = your cohort |
| **Status filtering** | Add `&clinical-status=active` to Condition queries if you want only current diagnoses; omit for full history |
| **Chemo adherence focus** | Add `&status=active` to MedicationRequest (query 2.1) to isolate currently active prescriptions |
| **Pagination** | Add `&_count=100` and follow `Bundle.link[next]` for large cohorts |
| **Date scoping** | Add `&onset-date=ge2020-01-01` (Condition) or `&date=ge2020-01-01` (Observation/Procedure) to scope to relevant time window |
| **Missing patients** | Patients documented only by medication or CEA (no formal Dx code) will only appear in Category 2/4 — validate clinically before inclusion |

## 📋 Implementation Notes

| Consideration | Guidance |
|---|---|
| **Union deduplication** | Collect all `subject` / `patient` references from returned bundles; deduplicate by Patient ID to form the final cohort |
| **Pagination** | Add `&_count=200` and follow `Bundle.link[relation="next"]` for large populations |
| **Inactive diagnoses** | Queries intentionally omit `clinical-status=active` to capture historical/resolved CRC diagnoses (relevant for surveillance cohort) |
| **Code validation** | RxNorm ingredient codes and SNOMED codes should be validated against your EHR's terminology server — some systems store product-level RxNorm codes instead of ingredient-level |
| **Medication references** | If medications are stored as `Medication` references rather than inline `CodeableConcept`, supplement with: `MedicationRequest?medication.code=<rxnorm-codes>` |
| **CEA unit normalization** | Some labs report in `µg/L` (numerically equivalent to `ng/mL`) — add a parallel query with `value-quantity=gt5\|http://unitsofmeasure.org\|ug/L` if needed |

## 🔁 Patient-Level Deduplication Strategy

After executing all 9 queries above, deduplicate by extracting the patient reference from each returned resource:

```
# Extract patient references from each bundle:
Condition      → Condition.subject.reference
MedicationRequest  → MedicationRequest.subject.reference
MedicationStatement → MedicationStatement.subject.reference
Procedure      → Procedure.subject.reference
Observation    → Observation.subject.reference
```

Then take the **UNION** of all unique `Patient/[id]` values across all queries. Patients appearing in any one query are included in the CRC population cohort.

## 📊 Summary of Validated Counts

| # | Signal | Resource | Codes Used | Live Count |
|---|--------|----------|------------|------------|
| 1 | CRC Diagnosis | `Condition` | 22 SNOMED + 11 ICD-10-CM + 11 ICD-9-CM | **70** |
| 2 | CRC Chemotherapy | `MedicationRequest` | 6 RxNorm ingredient codes | **70** |
| 3 | Colonoscopy | `Procedure` | 14 SNOMED + 13 CPT | **27** |
| 4 | Elevated CEA >5 ng/mL | `Observation` | 1 LOINC + value threshold | **35** |

## Key Design Decisions

1. **Triple-coded Conditions:** Live sampling confirmed this server encodes every CRC condition with SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM simultaneously — all three systems are included to future-proof the query if coding patterns change.

2. **Ingredient-level RxNorm for chemo:** Using the "ingredient" tier (e.g., `4492` for fluorouracil) rather than dose-form or branded codes casts the widest net — it matches FOLFOX, FOLFIRI, XELOX, CAPOX, and FOLFOXIRI regimens automatically by matching the active ingredient regardless of specific formulation.

3. **Dual-coded Procedures:** The server encodes procedures with SNOMED + CPT in parallel; both are included to catch records entered via either pathway.

4. **CEA threshold:** The `>5 ng/mL` cutoff uses the standard NCCN/ASCO CRC surveillance threshold. The `||ng/mL` unit qualifier is included to prevent unit-mismatch false negatives.

5. **Union strategy:** The four queries are intended to be unioned at the `Patient` reference level — a patient who appears in any of the four result sets is included in the CRC cohort.

2-broad.prompt_text:
I need to identify my complete colorectal cancer patient population for a quality improvement initiative on chemotherapy adherence and surveillance. From clinical experience, CRC patients are documented in different ways: most have a formal diagnosis (often anatomic-location-specific), most are on chemotherapy regimens (5-FU, capecitabine, oxaliplatin, irinotecan, leucovorin), most have undergone colonoscopy for diagnosis, and many have elevated CEA on surveillance.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any colorectal cancer diagnosis (colon, cecum, rectum, primary colon, descending colon)
2. Patients prescribed CRC chemotherapy (5-FU, capecitabine, oxaliplatin, irinotecan, leucovorin)
3. Patients who have undergone colonoscopy
4. Patients with elevated CEA (>5 ng/mL)

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found.

2-naive.prompt_text:
I need to identify my complete colorectal cancer patient population for a quality improvement initiative on chemotherapy adherence and surveillance. From clinical experience, CRC patients are documented in different ways: most have a formal diagnosis (often anatomic-location-specific), most are on chemotherapy regimens (5-FU, capecitabine, oxaliplatin, irinotecan, leucovorin), most have undergone colonoscopy for diagnosis, and many have elevated CEA on surveillance.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any colorectal cancer diagnosis (colon, cecum, rectum, primary colon, descending colon)
2. Patients prescribed CRC chemotherapy (5-FU, capecitabine, oxaliplatin, irinotecan, leucovorin)
3. Patients who have undergone colonoscopy
4. Patients with elevated CEA (>5 ng/mL)

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found.

3-broad.prompt_text: Find all patients with a diagnosis of colorectal cancer who also have evidence of surgical procedures, chemotherapy, or radiation therapy for cancer treatment.

3-expert.raw_response:
All codes validated. Now I have the complete picture:
- **27 Procedures** (all colonoscopies — no surgery/radiation procedures on this server)
- **70 MedicationRequests** (exclusively CRC chemotherapy drugs: fluorouracil, capecitabine, irinotecan, oxaliplatin, cetuximab, leucovorin)
- `_has` cross-resource AND returns **70 patients** for CRC dx + chemo

The final queries use **Playbook 11 (`_has` cross-resource AND)** × **Playbook 1 (full CRC subtype list)** × **Playbook 12 (validated case)**. Two lines for the three treatment arms (chemo via MedicationRequest; surgery + radiation via Procedure); evaluator unions the sets.

### What was done & why

| Decision | Evidence |
|---|---|
| **Playbooks 1+11+12** | CRC has many subtypes; treatment requires cross-resource AND (dx + treatment); "also have evidence" = validated case pattern |
| **CRC dx codes** | 15 SNOMED subtypes from ASCO VSAC OID `2.16.840.1.113883.3.1444.3.215` + 12 ICD-10-CM codes (C18–C20) from CancerLinQ OID |
| **Chemotherapy → MedicationRequest** | Server sampling revealed all 70 MedReqs are CRC drugs (RxNorm): fluorouracil `4492`, capecitabine `194000`, irinotecan `51499`, oxaliplatin `32592`, cetuximab `318341`, leucovorin `6313` |
| **Surgery → Procedure (CPT)** | Partial/total colectomy CPT 44140–44212; proctectomy CPT 45110–45111 (VSAC `Colectomy_CPT` confirmed) |
| **Radiation → Procedure (CPT + SNOMED)** | CPT 77261–77427 (VSAC `Radiation Therapy Indicators from CPT`); SNOMED `108290001` (Radiation oncology/radiotherapy) |
| **`_has` validated** | `Patient?_has:Condition:patient:code=...` returned 26→70 patients confirming reverse chaining works on this server |
| **Two lines = OR** | The evaluator unions the patient sets; any CRC patient with *any* treatment modality is captured |

1-naive.raw_response:
Here are the FHIR queries to find all patients with colorectal cancer, covering both SNOMED CT and ICD-10-CM code systems, along with patient inclusion:

**SNOMED CT — Colorectal Cancer:**
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|781382000&_include=Condition:subject
```

**SNOMED CT — Malignant Neoplasm of Colon:**
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|363346000&_include=Condition:subject
```

**SNOMED CT — Malignant Neoplasm of Rectum:**
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|363351006&_include=Condition:subject
```

**ICD-10-CM — Malignant Neoplasm of Colon (C18):**
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C18&_include=Condition:subject
```

**ICD-10-CM — Malignant Neoplasm of Rectosigmoid Junction (C19):**
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C19&_include=Condition:subject
```

**ICD-10-CM — Malignant Neoplasm of Rectum (C20):**
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C20&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### Notes:
| Code | System | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `781382000` | SNOMED CT | Malignant tumor of colorectum |
| `363346000` | SNOMED CT | Malignant neoplasm of colon |
| `363351006` | SNOMED CT | Malignant neoplasm of rectum |
| `C18` | ICD-10-CM | Malignant neoplasm of colon |
| `C19` | ICD-10-CM | Rectosigmoid junction |
| `C20` | ICD-10-CM | Malignant neoplasm of rectum |

- `_include=Condition:subject` fetches the related `Patient` resources in the same Bundle.
- You can add `&clinical-status=active` to filter for currently active diagnoses only.
- If your server supports it, you can combine codes in a single query using comma separation: `code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C18,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C19,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C20`.

1-broad.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries — Colorectal Cancer Diagnoses

### ICD-10-CM Based Query
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C18,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C18.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C18.6,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C18.9,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C19,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C20&_include=Condition:subject
```

### SNOMED CT Based Query
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|93761005,http://snomed.info/sct|94381002,http://snomed.info/sct|94261002,http://snomed.info/sct|187757001,http://snomed.info/sct|93854006&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### Code Reference Table

| Description | ICD-10-CM | SNOMED CT |
|---|---|---|
| Malignant neoplasm of colon (parent/unspecified) | `C18` / `C18.9` | `93761005` |
| Malignant neoplasm of cecum | `C18.0` | `94381002` |
| Malignant neoplasm of descending colon | `C18.6` | `94261002` |
| Malignant neoplasm of rectosigmoid junction | `C19` | `187757001` |
| Malignant neoplasm of rectum | `C20` | `93854006` |

### Notes
- **`_include=Condition:subject`** pulls the referenced `Patient` resources in the same bundle, satisfying the "find all patients" requirement
- Both coding systems are queried separately to maximize coverage across EHR systems that may encode diagnoses differently
- `C18` (category parent) and `C18.9` (unspecified) are both included since some systems record the parent ICD-10 block code rather than a specific sub-code
1-broad.prompt_text: Find all patients diagnosed with any form of colorectal cancer — including malignant neoplasm of the colon (parent), cecum, rectum and rectosigmoid junction, primary malignant neoplasm of colon, and primary malignant neoplasm of descending colon.

1-expert.raw_response:
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|363406005,http://snomed.info/sct|363350007,http://snomed.info/sct|187760008,http://snomed.info/sct|93761005,http://snomed.info/sct|93771007
```

**Explanation:**

| SNOMED Code | Description |
|---|---|
| `363406005` | Malignant neoplasm of colon *(parent concept)* |
| `363350007` | Malignant neoplasm of cecum |
| `187760008` | Malignant neoplasm of rectum |
| `93761005` | Primary malignant neoplasm of colon |
| `93771007` | Primary malignant neoplasm of descending colon |

**Design notes:**
- A **single query** with comma-separated values is used — FHIR treats commas as **OR logic** on the `code` parameter, returning Conditions matching *any* of the five codes.
- All codes are prefixed with `http://snomed.info/sct|` to scope the search strictly to SNOMED CT, preventing false matches from other code systems that may reuse those numeric values.
- To also include linked patient demographics or encounters, append `&_include=Condition:patient` or `&_include=Condition:encounter` as needed.
- If your server supports `ValueSet/$expand` + `code:in`, you could alternatively reference the VSAC OID directly: `Condition?code:in=http://cts.nlm.nih.gov/fhir/ValueSet/2.16.840.1.113883.3.1444.3.215`

1-naive.raw_response:
## FHIR Query: High CEA (Carcinoembryonic Antigen)

**CEA LOINC Code:** `2857-1` — Carcinoembryonic antigen [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma
**"High" threshold:** > 5 ng/mL (common clinical upper limit; non-smokers: >2.5, smokers: >5)

---

### Query — High CEA Observations (> 5 ng/mL)
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2857-1&value-quantity=gt5||ng/mL&_include=Observation:patient
```

### Query — Broader threshold (> 2.5 ng/mL, for non-smokers)
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2857-1&value-quantity=gt2.5||ng/mL&_include=Observation:patient
```

### Query — Flagged as abnormal (interpretation-based)
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2857-1&interpretation=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/v3-ObservationInterpretation|H&_include=Observation:patient
```

---

### Notes
| Detail | Value |
|---|---|
| **LOINC** | `2857-1` |
| **Normal range** | < 2.5 ng/mL (non-smokers), < 5 ng/mL (smokers) |
| **Elevated concern** | > 10 ng/mL often warrants oncologic workup |
| **Equivalent units** | `ng/mL` = `µg/L` |
| **Use case** | Colorectal cancer screening, monitoring, and recurrence detection |

> 💡 Use the **interpretation-based query** if your FHIR server stores pre-calculated H/L flags from the lab system, as unit representations may vary (ng/mL vs µg/L).
1-naive.prompt_text: Find patients with high CEA.

1-broad.raw_response:
## FHIR Query: Elevated CEA (> 5 ng/mL)

**CEA LOINC Code:** `2857-1` — *Carcinoembryonic antigen [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma*

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2857-1&value-quantity=gt5||ng/mL&status=final&_include=Observation:subject
```

---

### Handling Equivalent Units

CEA may be reported in **ng/mL** or the numerically equivalent **µg/L** — both queries should be used:

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2857-1&value-quantity=gt5||ng/mL&status=final&_include=Observation:subject
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2857-1&value-quantity=gt5||ug/L&status=final&_include=Observation:subject
```

---

### Notes

| Parameter | Detail |
|---|---|
| **LOINC Code** | `2857-1` — CEA in Serum/Plasma (most common) |
| **Threshold** | `gt5` (> 5, not ≥ 5) per standard CRC surveillance cutoff |
| **Units** | `ng/mL` ≡ `ug/L` (1:1 equivalence in UCUM) |
| **`_include=Observation:subject`** | Pulls associated `Patient` resources in the same bundle |
| **`status=final`** | Excludes preliminary/entered-in-error results |

> ⚠️ Some systems may also use LOINC `19167-6` (body fluid) or local/proprietary codes for CEA — consider querying your terminology server for all active mappings if results seem incomplete.
1-broad.prompt_text: Find patients with elevated Carcinoembryonic Antigen (CEA) — values greater than 5 ng/mL, the standard surveillance threshold for colorectal cancer recurrence.

1-expert.raw_response:
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2857-1&value-quantity=gt5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|ng/mL&status=final,amended&_include=Observation:subject
```

---

### Query Breakdown

| Component | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| **Resource** | `Observation` | Lab results live here in FHIR |
| **LOINC Code** | `2857-1` | *Carcinoembryonic antigen [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma* — the canonical CEA panel code |
| **value-quantity** | `gt5` | Threshold of **>5 ng/mL**; values in this range warrant clinical investigation for CRC recurrence/metastasis |
| **Unit system** | `http://unitsofmeasure.org\|ng/mL` | UCUM-coded unit anchors the numeric comparison to the correct scale |
| **status** | `final,amended` | Excludes preliminary/entered-in-error/cancelled results; includes corrected (amended) reports |
| **_include** | `Observation:subject` | Pulls the linked `Patient` resource in one round-trip for downstream patient-level work |

---

### Clinical & Data Caveats

- **Smokers** — CEA can be chronically mildly elevated (5–10 ng/mL) without malignancy; smoking status should be cross-referenced.
- **Inflammatory bowel disease, liver disease, pancreatitis** — can cause non-malignant elevations; combine with `Condition` queries for CRC ICD-10 codes (`C18–C20`) to improve specificity.
- **Trending matters** — a single elevated value is less actionable than a rising serial CEA; consider querying multiple time-stamped observations per patient using `subject` chaining.
- **Unit variability** — some labs may report in `ug/L` (numerically equivalent to ng/mL); if results appear missing, add a second query substituting `|ug/L` as the unit.

This requires cross-referencing **colon cancer diagnoses**, **active chemotherapy medications**, and **chemotherapy procedures**.

### 1. 🔍 Patient-Level Query (Combined via `_has`)

**With active chemo procedure:**
```
Patient?_has:Condition:patient:code=http://snomed.info/sct|363406005,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C18.9&_has:Procedure:patient:code=http://snomed.info/sct|367336001&_has:Procedure:patient:status=in-progress
```

**With active chemo medication:**
```
Patient?_has:Condition:patient:code=http://snomed.info/sct|363406005,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C18.9&_has:MedicationRequest:patient:status=active&_has:MedicationRequest:patient:code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|4492,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|151677,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|72962,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|194000,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|358255,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|318341
```

### Query Breakdown

| RxNorm Code | Drug | CRC Specificity |
|---|---|---|
| `4492` | Fluorouracil (5-FU) | Core CRC agent; also used in gastric/pancreatic/other GI cancers |
| `194000` | Capecitabine (oral 5-FU prodrug) | Core CRC agent; cross-indication with gastric, breast |
| `32592` | Oxaliplatin | Highly CRC-specific (FOLFOX, CAPOX/XELOX) |
| `51499` | Irinotecan | Highly CRC-specific (FOLFIRI, FOLFIRINOX) |
| `6313` | Leucovorin (folinic acid) | CRC modulator (FOLFOX/FOLFIRI); also used in methotrexate rescue |

### Design Notes
- **Comma-separation** acts as an **OR** across all five ingredient codes in a single request, minimizing round trips.
- These are **ingredient-level** RxNorm codes (`IN` term type), so they will match regardless of formulation, dose form, or brand name — ensure your FHIR server supports ingredient-level code matching (not all do; some only match at `SCD`/`SBD` level).
- **Leucovorin** (`6313`) will have the most false positives for non-CRC indications (e.g., methotrexate toxicity rescue). Consider filtering results by `reasonCode` or chaining with a CRC `Condition` if precision is needed:

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|4492,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|194000,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|32592,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|51499,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|6313&reason-code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C18,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C19,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|C20
```

1-naive.raw_response:
Here are the FHIR queries to find patients with colonoscopies across relevant resource types:

```
Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|73761001&_include=Procedure:patient
DiagnosticReport?code=http://loinc.org|18746-8&_include=DiagnosticReport:patient
ServiceRequest?code=http://snomed.info/sct|73761001&_include=ServiceRequest:patient
```

**Explanation:**

| Query | Resource | Code System | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Procedure` | Completed/performed procedures | SNOMED CT `73761001` (Colonoscopy) | Most common source |
| `DiagnosticReport` | Colonoscopy reports/findings | LOINC `18746-8` (Colonoscopy study) | Captures documented reports |
| `ServiceRequest` | Ordered colonoscopies | SNOMED CT `73761001` | Captures requested/planned procedures |

- **`_include=...:patient`** pulls back the associated `Patient` resources in the same bundle.
- If you only want **completed** colonoscopies, add `&status=completed` to the `Procedure` query.
- If you want to search by patient directly, replace `_include` with `_revinclude` on a `Patient` query, or chain the results.

1-expert.raw_response:
```
Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|73761001&status=completed
```

**SNOMED CT Code Used:**
| Code | Display | System |
|------|---------|--------|
| `73761001` | Colonoscopy | SNOMED CT |

**Query Design Notes:**
- **Resource:** `Procedure` — as specified, targeting procedural records only (not Condition or MedicationRequest)
- **`status=completed`** — filters to procedures that were actually performed (excludes cancelled, entered-in-error, etc.), ensuring clinical validity
- **SNOMED CT `73761001`** — the canonical concept for *Colonoscopy*, covering both **diagnostic** (follow-up on symptoms/findings) and **screening** (routine CRC surveillance) contexts
- This complements the colorectal cancer cohort identification pipeline alongside:
  - **Diagnosis path** → `Condition?code` (ICD-10 CRC codes)
  - **Tumor marker path** → `Observation?code` (e.g., LOINC CEA codes)

**Optional Enhancements:**
- Add `_include=Procedure:patient` to pull associated Patient resources in one request
- Add a `date` filter (e.g., `&date=ge2021-01-01`) to scope to recent colonoscopies for active surveillance cohorts
- Consider additional SNOMED children/synonyms (e.g., `174158000` — *Colonoscopy and biopsy*) if your terminology server does not perform subsumption by default
